questions.question_text: When is the driver responsible for ensuring that passengers are wearing seat belts?

answers.1: I am responsible for ensuring that all passengers under 15 years of age are correctly fastened with a seat belt.
answers.2: I am responsible only for passengers in the front seat.
answers.3: If a seat belt is not used, the risk of being thrown out of the vehicle in an accident increases.
answers.4: While driving, the airbag may be directly life-threatening if a seat belt is not used.

questions.question_text: What am I permitted to tow behind a passenger car?

answers.1: I may tow a sled.
answers.2: I may tow a caravan.
answers.3: I may tow a cart.

questions.question_text: What is the highest permitted alcohol concentration in the breath when driving a passenger car?

answers.1: 0.25 milligrams per litre of breath.
answers.2: 0.50 milligrams per litre of breath.
answers.3: 0.75 milligrams per litre of breath.

questions.question_text: How many penalty points must a new driver receive within 3 years to lose the licence?

answers.1: 1 penalty point.
answers.2: 2 penalty points.
answers.3: 3 penalty points.

questions.question_text: How can I drive in a more environmentally friendly manner and reduce CO2 emissions?

answers.1: I switch off the air conditioning when it is not necessary.
answers.2: I start the engine immediately before driving.
answers.3: Driving with low tyre pressure reduces energy consumption.

questions.question_text: Why is the exhaust system of the passenger car important?

answers.1: It makes the passenger car faster.
answers.2: It reduces fuel consumption.
answers.3: It reduces the pollution from exhaust gases.
answers.4: Leaks in the exhaust system may allow dangerous, odourless carbon monoxide to enter the passenger car.

questions.question_text: Who must I show special consideration for in traffic?

answers.1: Only children, because they have less experience in traffic.
answers.2: Only elderly and disabled persons.
answers.3: Children, school patrols, elderly persons, and disabled persons.
answers.4: I need to show special consideration only for bicyclists and pedestrians.

questions.question_text: What applies if the ABS warning light does not go out shortly after starting the passenger car?

answers.1: There may be a fault in the ABS braking system, and I may drive only to the nearest workshop.
answers.2: The ABS braking system is functioning correctly, and there is no cause for concern.
answers.3: The ABS braking system is temporarily deactivated because of low battery voltage.

questions.question_text: Which of the following statements about the legal requirements for car lights are correct?

answers.1: The high-beam headlights must illuminate the road at least 100 metres ahead of the passenger car.
answers.2: The high-beam headlights must illuminate the road at least 200 metres ahead of the passenger car.
answers.3: The low-beam headlights must illuminate the road at least 30 metres ahead of the passenger car without dazzling others.
answers.4: The low-beam headlights must illuminate the road at least 50 metres ahead of the passenger car without dazzling others.

questions.question_text: Which rules apply to carrying an approved warning triangle in the passenger car?

answers.1: I have a duty to carry an approved warning triangle in the passenger car at all times.
answers.2: A warning triangle is necessary only when driving outside built-up areas.
answers.3: A warning triangle is required only when driving a truck or a bus.
answers.4: While driving, I should carry an approved warning triangle for situations where I have a duty to place it.

questions.question_text: Which general speed limits apply to passenger cars with a trailer up to 3,500 kg?

answers.1: In built-up areas, the maximum speed is normally 30 km/h.
answers.2: In built-up areas, the maximum speed is normally 50 km/h.
answers.3: Outside built-up areas, the maximum speed is normally 80 km/h.
answers.4: With special approval, a passenger car with a trailer may be permitted to drive at 100 km/h on the motorway.

questions.question_text: What applies to the total permitted weight of the passenger car and trailer?

answers.1: It must not exceed 3,500 kg.
answers.2: It must not exceed 2,800 kg.
answers.3: It must not exceed 4,500 kg.

questions.question_text: Which of the following statements about eco-driving is correct?

answers.1: I must operate the passenger car in a manner that avoids unnecessary noise and excessive exhaust fumes.
answers.2: Driving fast in a fuel-consuming passenger car can save fuel.
answers.3: Letting the passenger car idle while waiting is good practice.

questions.question_text: Which of the following statements about the registration certificate are correct?

answers.1: The passenger car's curb weight or ready-to-drive weight is stated.
answers.2: The maximum weight the passenger car is allowed to carry is stated.
answers.3: The permitted total weight is stated.
answers.4: The permitted trailer weight is stated.

questions.question_text: When may I use rear fog lights?

answers.1: I may use them when it is very foggy and only if this does not inconvenience other road users.
answers.2: I may use them in all weather conditions to increase the visibility of the passenger car.
answers.3: I may use them during heavy rain or snowfall.

questions.question_text: Which rules apply to marking a load?

answers.1: A load extending more than 1 metre beyond the front or rear of the vehicle must be clearly marked with a cloth or in another visible way.
answers.2: A load extending more than 15 cm beyond the sides must be clearly marked with a cloth or in another visible way.
answers.3: During lighting-up time, the load must be marked with a white cylinder and lights - red at the rear and white at the front.
answers.4: During lighting-up time, the load must be marked with lights, but not necessarily with a cylinder.

questions.question_text: Which rules apply to the distance between vehicles during towing?

answers.1: The maximum distance between vehicles during towing is 4 metres.
answers.2: If the distance between the vehicles exceeds 2 metres, the rope must be clearly marked.
answers.3: The law permits a distance of up to 5 metres between vehicles during towing.
answers.4: The distance between the vehicles must not exceed 3 metres.

questions.question_text: Which rules apply to placing a warning triangle in the event of a breakdown or accident?

answers.1: On motorways, the warning triangle must be placed at least 50 metres before the vehicle in the event of a breakdown or accident.
answers.2: On motorways, the warning triangle must be placed at least 100 metres before the vehicle in the event of a breakdown or accident.
answers.3: On ordinary roads, the warning triangle must be placed at least 50 metres before the vehicle if it poses a danger or obstruction.
answers.4: On ordinary roads, the warning triangle must be placed at least 100 metres before the vehicle if it poses a danger.

questions.question_text: Which rules apply when towing vehicles?

answers.1: It is permitted to tow a vehicle when the person in the towed vehicle is at least 18 years old, even if that person does not hold the required driving licence.
answers.2: The driver of the towed vehicle must hold a valid driving licence for that vehicle.
answers.3: The maximum speed while towing must not exceed 30 km/h.
answers.4: If the towed vehicle has functioning brakes, the speed may be increased to 50 km/h if necessary.

questions.question_text: Which of the following statements is correct regarding the rules on intoxicating substances and drugs?

answers.1: It is illegal to let someone drive my passenger car if that person has taken intoxicating substances.
answers.2: The police may require saliva or sweat tests and an eye examination only if they suspect drug influence.
answers.3: The police may require a driver to take saliva or sweat tests or undergo an eye examination at any time.

questions.question_text: Which rules apply in the event of an accident?

answers.1: I must stop immediately and assist injured persons if I am involved in a traffic accident, regardless of fault.
answers.2: I need to stop and assist only if the accident was my fault.
answers.3: I must provide my name and address to others involved if requested.
answers.4: I need to provide my name and address only to the police or other authorities if requested.

questions.question_text: Which rules apply to braking distance for motor vehicles?

answers.1: The braking distance is the distance the passenger car travels from the moment braking begins until it comes to a complete stop.
answers.2: The braking distance depends, among other things, on how hard I press the brake pedal.
answers.3: If the speed is doubled, the braking distance will also double.
answers.4: If the speed is doubled, the braking distance will become four times as long.

questions.question_text: Which of the following statements about the use of the horn and light signals are correct?

answers.1: I may use the horn or flash the headlights to warn of danger.
answers.2: During lighting-up time, I must use light signals instead of the horn unless the danger is immediate.
answers.3: I may use the horn to attract attention even if there is no emergency.
answers.4: The horn may be used only as a warning of danger.

questions.question_text: Which of the following statements about alcohol rules and breath tests is correct?

answers.1: It is illegal to let someone drive my passenger car if that person has consumed so much alcohol that safe driving is not possible.
answers.2: The police may require a breath test from a driver at any time, but only if alcohol consumption is suspected.
answers.3: The police may require a breath test from a driver at any time.

questions.question_text: What requirement applies to liability insurance for a motor vehicle?

answers.1: The owner of the passenger car must have mandatory liability insurance covering damage caused to others.
answers.2: Mandatory liability insurance is not required if the passenger car is used only for private driving.
answers.3: Mandatory liability insurance is required only if the passenger car is used for work, such as a taxi.
